api/spec/packages/aip/src/shared.purpose: Shared TypeSpec primitives (scalars, base models, generic request/response envelopes, filters) reused across all aip/ v3 domain namespaces — the type-system foundation for the v3 API. Changes here propagate across every domain.

patterns.name: Scalar with validation constraints
patterns.description: New primitive types are TypeSpec scalars (not models) with @pattern, @minLength/@maxLength, @encode, @example, @friendlyName. ULID, ResourceKey, CurrencyCode, DateTime, ISO8601Duration, Numeric follow this.

patterns.name: @visibility lifecycle decorators on all model fields
patterns.description: Every field uses @visibility(Lifecycle.Create/Read/Update). Read-only fields (id, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at) carry only Lifecycle.Read.
patterns.example:
@visibility(Lifecycle.Read) id: ULID;
@visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update) name: string;

patterns.name: Generic request/response envelopes via templates
patterns.description: CreateRequest<T>, UpdateRequest<T>, UpsertRequest<T>, GetResponse<T>, CreateResponse<T>, CursorPaginatedResponse<T>, PagePaginatedResponse<T> are parameterized generics. Domain models spread into these instead of redeclaring status codes or pagination plumbing.

patterns.name: Resource base models spread into domain models
patterns.description: Resource (id, name, description, labels, timestamps), ResourceWithKey (adds key), ResourceImmutable (strips updated_at/deleted_at) are the only base models. Domain models spread one rather than redeclaring those fields.

patterns.name: Filter models with self-referential AND/OR recursion
patterns.description: Query filter models (QueryFilterString/Integer/Numeric/DateTime/Boolean) include and?/or? arrays of themselves; in/nin bounded by @maxItems(100), and/or by @maxItems(10). New filter types follow this shape.
patterns.example: model QueryFilterString { eq?: string; in?: string[]; @maxItems(10) and?: QueryFilterString[]; @maxItems(10) or?: QueryFilterString[]; }

patterns.name: index.tsp barrel — single import entry point
patterns.description: All sibling .tsp files are imported by index.tsp; consumers import only ../shared/index.tsp, never individual files. New files must be added to index.tsp.

patterns.name: Extension constants in consts.tsp
patterns.description: API tag names, descriptions, and extension keys (UnstableExtension, InternalExtension, PrivateExtension) are const in consts.tsp; operations reference Shared.<Const>, never inline the string.

key_file_guides.role: Shared scalar types (ULID, ResourceKey, DateTime, CurrencyCode, Numeric, ISO8601Duration) and value-object models (RecurringPeriod, ClosedPeriod, CurrencyAmount).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Numeric is a string scalar (arbitrary-precision) — never substitute float64/integer for monetary/quantity values. DateTime uses @encode(rfc3339), not a plain string.

key_file_guides.role: Resource, ResourceWithKey, ResourceImmutable base models and ResourceReference<T> for cross-domain references.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Spreading Resource brings all lifecycle timestamps automatically. Do not add domain-specific fields to base models. ResourceReference<T> contains only id: ULID.

key_file_guides.role: Generic Create/Update/Upsert request wrappers with @withVisibility filtering; CreateRequestNested handles nested-visibility models.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use CreateRequestNested (not plain CreateRequest) when the model has nested structs with Lifecycle decorators — plain CreateRequest misses nested visibility filtering.

key_file_guides.role: HTTP response envelopes with hardcoded status codes (GetResponse=200, CreateResponse=201, UpsertResponse=200, DeleteResponse=204); CursorPaginatedResponse/PagePaginatedResponse with @pageItems data array and meta.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Status codes are baked into the generics — do not create ad-hoc response models with @statusCode; always use these generics.

key_file_guides.role: Reusable query filter models for string/integer/float/numeric/boolean/datetime; QueryFilterStringMapItem extends QueryFilterString with exists? for map entries.
key_file_guides.watch_for: in/nin arrays @maxItems(100); and/or arrays @maxItems(10). All filters are self-referentially nested for logical composition.

key_file_guides.role: String constants for all API tags/descriptions (MetersTag, BillingTag, etc.) and extension keys (UnstableExtension, InternalExtension, PrivateExtension).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add new tag/description pairs here; do not inline strings in operations files.

anti_patterns: Redeclaring id/name/created_at/updated_at/deleted_at on a domain model — spread ...Shared.Resource or ...Shared.ResourceWithKey
Using float64 or integer for monetary/quantity values — use Shared.Numeric
Creating a filter model without and?/or? self-references when logical composition is needed
Defining HTTP status codes inline with @statusCode on domain response models — use the generic envelopes
Adding a new .tsp file to shared/ without importing it in index.tsp

decisions.decision: Scalars with inline validation constraints over Go-side validation in handlers
decisions.rationale: TypeSpec scalars emit JSON Schema constraints into OpenAPI, so all SDKs and kin-openapi/oasmiddleware validate automatically without duplicating rules in Go.

decisions.decision: Generic request/response templates instead of per-resource envelope models
decisions.rationale: Eliminates CRUD drift across 15+ domain namespaces; visibility filtering is applied once in the template and propagates everywhere.

code_examples.scenario: Defining a new domain resource extending shared base types with CRUD envelopes

api/spec/packages/aip/src/subscriptions.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the Subscriptions v3 API domain: subscription lifecycle model, CRUD+action operations (create, list, get, cancel, unschedule-cancelation, change), subscription addon model, and cross-domain SubscriptionReference. All @friendlyName values are 'Billing'-prefixed to avoid SDK type collisions.

patterns.name: BillingXxx friendlyName prefix on all types
patterns.description: Every model, enum, and union carries @friendlyName("BillingXxx") to avoid SDK type-name collisions with same-named types in billing/productcatalog namespaces. New types must follow the prefix.

patterns.name: Action operations as @route-decorated methods on the same interface
patterns.description: Non-CRUD actions (cancel, unschedule-cancelation, change) are methods on SubscriptionsOperations with an explicit @route("/{subscriptionId}/action-name"), not nested interfaces or extensions.
patterns.example: @post @route("/{subscriptionId}/cancel") @operationId("cancel-subscription") cancel(@path subscriptionId: Shared.ULID, @body body: SubscriptionCancel): Shared.UpdateResponse<Subscription> | ...

patterns.name: OmitProperties<> to derive create/change shapes from the read model
patterns.description: Request bodies omit server-assigned fields (billing_anchor, status) from the read model via OmitProperties<Subscription, ...>, then add explicit Create-only fields (customer, plan references) inline.
patterns.example: model SubscriptionCreate { ...Shared.CreateRequest<OmitProperties<Subscription, "customer_id" | "plan_id" | "billing_anchor">>; customer: { id?: Shared.ULID; key?: Shared.ExternalResourceKey; }; }

patterns.name: @oneOf union for flexible timing parameters
patterns.description: SubscriptionEditTiming is a @oneOf union of an enum (immediate/next_billing_cycle) and a raw DateTime, letting callers pass shorthand or an explicit timestamp without a discriminator field.
patterns.example: @oneOf @friendlyName("BillingSubscriptionEditTiming") union SubscriptionEditTiming { Enum: SubscriptionEditTimingEnum, Custom: Shared.DateTime }

patterns.name: Dual-subscription response for change operation
patterns.description: The change operation returns SubscriptionChangeResponse with both current and next Subscription fields — not wrapped in Shared.UpdateResponse<T> — because of its two-subscription payload.
patterns.example: @friendlyName("BillingSubscriptionChangeResponse") model SubscriptionChangeResponse { current: Subscription; next: Subscription; }

key_file_guides.role: Core domain model: Subscription (read), SubscriptionCreate, SubscriptionStatus enum, SubscriptionEditTiming union, SubscriptionEditTimingEnum enum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Subscription spreads ...OmitProperties<Shared.Resource, "name" | "description"> — name and description are intentionally absent. Do not add them back.

key_file_guides.role: SubscriptionsOperations interface with all HTTP operations including action routes; SubscriptionCancel and SubscriptionChange request models declared inline.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The change operation returns raw SubscriptionChangeResponse (not wrapped in Shared.UpdateResponse) with its own @Http.statusCode — do not wrap it.

key_file_guides.role: SubscriptionReference cross-domain type for billing line items pointing to a specific subscription phase+item.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Deeply nested (subscription -> phase -> item) using anonymous inline structs; do not extract inner structs into separate named models.

key_file_guides.role: SubscriptionAddon model: addon reference, quantity, quantity_at, active_from/active_to lifecycle times.
key_file_guides.watch_for: quantity_at is Read-only (server-resolved); active_from/active_to are Read-only temporal fields.

anti_patterns: Omitting @friendlyName("BillingXxx") from a new model/enum — generated SDK types would collide with same-named types in other namespaces
Wrapping the change operation response in Shared.UpdateResponse<T> — its two-subscription payload is intentional
Adding name or description fields to the Subscription model — explicitly omitted via OmitProperties
Defining a new action endpoint as a separate interface instead of a @route-decorated method on SubscriptionsOperations

decisions.rationale: Multiple aip/ namespaces (subscriptions, billing, productcatalog) expose similarly-named types; the prefix makes generated Go/JS SDK types unambiguous without package-qualified names.

decisions.decision: @oneOf union for SubscriptionEditTiming instead of separate fields
decisions.rationale: Lets callers use convenient enum values (immediate, next_billing_cycle) or an explicit timestamp without an extra discriminator field or endpoint variants.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new action endpoint (e.g. pause)

api/spec/packages/aip/src/tax.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the Tax Codes v3 API domain: full CRUD for TaxCode resources mapping OpenMeter app types (Stripe, Sandbox, etc.) to provider-specific tax codes. An internal/private API surface — all endpoints carry PrivateExtension, UnstableExtension, and InternalExtension.

patterns.name: Triple private/unstable/internal extension on all operations
patterns.description: Every operation in TaxCodesOperations carries @extension(Shared.PrivateExtension, true), @extension(Shared.UnstableExtension, true), and @extension(Shared.InternalExtension, true). New operations must carry all three.
patterns.example: @post @operationId("create-tax-code") @extension(Shared.InternalExtension, true) @extension(Shared.UnstableExtension, true) create(@body tax_code: Shared.CreateRequest<TaxCode>): Shared.CreateResponse<TaxCode> | Common.ErrorResponses;

patterns.name: Upsert (@put) over separate create+update for sync-friendly resources
patterns.description: Tax codes use @put upsert (Shared.UpsertRequest<T> body, Shared.UpsertResponse<T> + Common.Gone) rather than separate create/update, because tax codes are synced idempotently from external provider catalogs.
patterns.example: @put @operationId("upsert-tax-code") upsert(@path taxCodeId: Shared.ULID, @body tax_code: Shared.UpsertRequest<TaxCode>): Shared.UpsertResponse<TaxCode> | Common.Gone | Common.NotFound | Common.ErrorResponses;

patterns.name: Cross-namespace import of a specific .tsp file (not index)
patterns.description: codes.tsp imports only ../apps/app.tsp (not ../apps/index.tsp) to reference Apps.AppType. Import the specific .tsp file to minimize transitive dependencies.
patterns.example:
import "../apps/app.tsp";
model TaxCodeAppMapping { app_type: Apps.AppType; tax_code: string; }

patterns.name: ResourceWithKey for key-addressable resources
patterns.description: TaxCode spreads ...Shared.ResourceWithKey (not plain Resource) because tax codes are addressable by human-readable key in addition to ULID.

key_file_guides.role: TaxCode resource model and TaxCodeAppMapping value-object; TaxCodeReference extends Shared.ResourceReference<TaxCode>.
key_file_guides.watch_for: app_mappings carries @visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Update, Lifecycle.Create) — required on both create and update. Do not make it optional without backward-compatibility analysis.

key_file_guides.role: TaxCodesOperations interface: create, get, list (with include_deleted query param), upsert, delete.
key_file_guides.watch_for: List includes @query include_deleted?: boolean (soft-delete). Upsert can return Common.Gone (410) if the resource was hard-deleted — the Go handler must handle this.

key_file_guides.role: Barrel: imports codes.tsp then operations.tsp in dependency order.
key_file_guides.watch_for: codes.tsp must be imported before operations.tsp since operations references TaxCode.

anti_patterns: Adding a new endpoint without all three extensions (Private/Unstable/Internal) — this is a private internal API
Using Shared.Resource instead of Shared.ResourceWithKey — tax codes are key-addressable
Splitting upsert into separate create+update endpoints — idempotent upsert is required for the sync job pattern
Importing ../apps/index.tsp instead of ../apps/app.tsp — import only the specific file containing the needed type

decisions.decision: Upsert (@put) instead of separate create/update for tax codes
decisions.rationale: Tax codes are synced from external provider catalogs; idempotent upsert avoids 409 conflicts on repeated sync runs and simplifies the sync job.
Tax code management is an operator-level function not exposed in the public SDK; the extensions exclude these endpoints from customer-facing SDK generation and docs.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new TaxCode field following existing visibility patterns
